---
title: Issue types
description: The kinds of work a project supports — fields, documents, icons, parent/child rules.
---

An **issue type** is the shape of a piece of work. A bug looks different from a contract looks different from a change request — different fields, different required attachments, different child-issue rules. Issue types live in `.kiket/issue_types.yaml` and a project can declare as many as it needs.

## File location

`.kiket/issue_types.yaml` — one file per project, all types listed together.

## Minimum file

```yaml
types:
  task:
    label: Task
    icon: circle
    color: slate

  bug:
    label: Bug
    icon: bug
    color: rose

  feature:
    label: Feature
    icon: sparkles
    color: indigo
```

Three buttons in the "New issue" dialog, each producing a differently-tinted card.

## Custom fields

Each type can declare additional fields beyond the standard ones (title, description, assignee, priority, labels, due date):

```yaml
types:
  contract:
    label: Contract
    icon: file-signature
    fields:
      - key: counterparty
        label: Counterparty
        type: text
        required: true
      - key: total_value_usd
        label: Total value (USD)
        type: number
      - key: start_date
        label: Start date
        type: date
      - key: contract_type
        label: Type
        type: select
        options: [msa, sow, nda, other]
```

Field types match those in [intake forms](/docs/build/intake-forms#field-types).

## Required documents

Some issue types need specific attachments before certain transitions:

```yaml
types:
  contract:
    documents:
      - key: draft_contract
        label: Draft contract PDF
        required: true
        accept: application/pdf
      - key: signed_nda
        label: Signed NDA
        required: false
        accept: application/pdf
```

`required: true` *declares* what's expected; whether a transition is *blocked* by missing documents is set per state in the workflow (`required_documents: [draft_contract]`).

## Parent / child rules

Issues can have parent/child relationships (epics → stories, contracts → amendments):

```yaml
types:
  epic:
    allow_children: true
    child_types: [story, task]

  story:
    parent_types: [epic]
    allow_children: true
    child_types: [task]

  task:
    parent_types: [epic, story]
```

The API refuses to create a parent/child pair that violates the declared rules.

## Icon + color

Icons reference the [Lucide library](https://lucide.dev). Colors are one of our tone names (slate, indigo, violet, fuchsia, emerald, amber, rose, cyan) — the card border and icon badge pick them up.

## Editing surfaces

The **Issue types** page in settings is a visual editor over this file. Every change commits immediately.
